message.content:
@startuml
class Artisan {
    + salaire
    + coordonnee
    + getPaye()
}

class Travailler {
    + nbHeure
    + debMission
    + finMission
    + calculerPaye()
}

class Chantier {
    + dateD
    + dateF
    + adresse
    + getAdresse()
    + affecterArtisan()
}

' Relationships based on visual connections in the provided image.
% Note: The original diagram shows an unusual connection style where 'Travailler' connects to the line between 'Artisan' and 'Chantier'. 
In standard UML/PlantUML, this represents associations involving all three classes or specific binary relationships inferred from context. Here we model them as direct links for clarity while preserving intent visually implied by proximity/layout if needed via positioning hints below but primarily focusing on structural correctness of entities & their attributes/methods first then linking logically per typical domain understanding unless explicitly stated otherwise which isn't here beyond what’s drawn; thus assuming logical groupings around work assignments etc., though strictly speaking without further textual clarification one might interpret differently — however given constraints let's proceed representing each box accurately along lines connecting nearest neighbors directly since exact semantics aren’t fully specified apart form graphical layout alone): 

Artisan "1..*" -- "*" Chantier : > 0..*  
note right of Artisan::getPaye(): Calculates payment details\nbased upon hours worked during missions assigned through chantier engagements
